Player at (24, 28), center frame at (28, 32). The frame occupies cols 32-35. The player occupies cols 28-31. So the player is just to the left of the frame. I need to move RIGHT to get to col 32, then DOWN to get below the frame (row > 31), then SPACE, then UP to collect.
Wait, looking at the image, the player is to the left of the center frame. The frame is at (28, 32) with boundary [[28, 32], [28, 35], [31, 35], [31, 32]], so it occupies rows 28-31, cols 32-34 (width 3).
Player at (24, 28) with boundary [[24, 28], [24, 31], [26, 31], [26, 28]], so rows 24-26, cols 28-30 (width 3).
I need to move RIGHT to align with the frame (col 32), then DOWN to get below it. Let me move RIGHT first.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at (24, 28), frame at (28, 32)
# Move RIGHT to align, then DOWN to get below frame
action(['RIGHT', 'DOWN', 'DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
